mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or forward returns are negative in >55% of rate-cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off selling and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in >60% of instances where UNRATE rises above the 0.4pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based domestic demand recovery, driving positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce positive 60-day returns in >50% of cases, or interaction term loses statistical significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of industrial production contraction, a rebound in the index signals supply-side stabilization that removes recession fears and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO turns positive after 3 consecutive months of decline
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns remain negative or flat in >65% of confirmed INDPRO rebound instances within the specified regime.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls consistently reflect strengthening economic activity and corporate earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Rank IC drops below 0.01 over rolling 2-year windows or positive payroll surprises yield negative 5-day returns in >55% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: When core inflation measured by CPI exceeds 3.0 percent annually, anticipated aggressive monetary tightening compresses equity multiples and forces negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 60-day returns in >60% of periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds the 3.0% annual threshold.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where real GDP growth accelerates while housing starts decline indicates a shift toward services and financial sector strength, generating positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The GDPC1-HOUST divergence signal produces negative 20-day returns in >55% of cases or fails to outperform a baseline market-neutral strategy.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation environment, a sudden drop in industrial production confirms supply chain bottlenecks and stagflation risks, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: Negative 5-day returns occur in <40% of late-cycle INDPRO decline episodes, or the signal loses predictive power when CPIAUCSL is below 2.0%.
